entry.headline: Merrill and BofA Private Bank Deploy AI-Powered End-to-End Meeting Workflow Across Advisor Force
entry.the_so_what: Bank of America deploying an AI meeting workflow across both Merrill and the Private Bank simultaneously signals that the pre/during/post-meeting automation stack is now a baseline infrastructure expectation at scale — following the same pattern as CRM adoption in the early 2000s, where the wirehouses that embedded workflow tooling earliest compounded productivity advantages that independent channels took a decade to close.
entry.company_name: Bank of America / Merrill / Bank of America Private Bank
entry.summary: Merrill and Bank of America Private Bank have launched an AI-powered Meeting Journey tool designed to support advisors across the full client meeting lifecycle — preparation, in-meeting assistance, and post-meeting follow-up. The capability spans both the Merrill wealth management platform and the Private Bank, indicating a unified deployment across the firm's primary client-facing advisor channels. The product launch was announced by Bank of America Corporation on March 26, 2026. No specific advisor headcount, AUM affected, or time-savings metric was disclosed in the source article.

capability_evidence.evidence: Bank of America announced the live launch of an AI-powered Meeting Journey tool across both Merrill and Bank of America Private Bank, covering pre-meeting preparation, in-meeting support, and post-meeting workflow.

entry.headline: Merrill and BofA Private Bank Deploy AI Meeting Journey Across Advisor Workforce
entry.the_so_what: Wirehouse-scale deployment of end-to-end AI meeting workflow — spanning pre-meeting prep, in-meeting guidance, and post-meeting follow-up — mirrors the CRM adoption curve of the early 2000s, where institutions that embedded workflow tools first accumulated structural data advantages that independent competitors spent a decade trying to replicate.
entry.company_name: Bank of America / Merrill / Bank of America Private Bank
entry.summary: Merrill and Bank of America Private Bank have launched an AI-powered Meeting Journey that automates and supports the full advisor-client meeting lifecycle — including pre-meeting preparation, real-time in-meeting assistance, and post-meeting follow-up and documentation. The tool is deployed across Merrill's advisor workforce and Bank of America Private Bank's client teams, making it a broad institutional rollout rather than a pilot. The capability is built to surface relevant client data, generate meeting summaries, and automate next-step workflows, reducing the administrative burden on advisors. The launch was announced on March 26, 2026, via Bank of America's official newsroom and distributed through PR Newswire.

capability_evidence.evidence: The AI-powered Meeting Journey is live across Merrill and Bank of America Private Bank, automating pre-meeting prep, in-meeting guidance, and post-meeting follow-up for advisors.

sources.name: Bank of America / Merrill / Bank of America Private Bank Newsroom

entry.headline: LLM-Powered Advisors Reach Mass Market as One-Third of Consumers Now Use AI for Investment Guidance
entry.the_so_what: Robinhood's Strategies product — 250,000 paying customers at $250/year handling concentrated stock positions and multi-goal planning — establishes a direct-to-consumer AI advice revenue model that incumbent wirehouses structurally cannot replicate without undermining the AUM-fee economics that underpin their entire high-net-worth business, while Anthropic's simultaneous embedding into LPL's advisor channel means AI is now compressing the $100K–$1M segment from both the consumer and the institutional supply side at once.
entry.summary: LLM-based financial tools have moved beyond robo-advisor-era ETF bucketing: Robinhood's Strategies product handles complex personalisation scenarios — concentrated stock option positions, home purchase planning — and has 250,000 paying customers at an average $250 per year. More than one-third of consumers across all age groups are now using tools like Claude and ChatGPT for investment guidance, often before meeting a human advisor, according to McKinsey. Anthropic has accelerated institutional distribution by partnering with LPL Financial (serving ~8 million end clients via tens of thousands of registered advisors) and ingesting financial data from S&P Global, Morningstar, and PitchBook, effectively embedding proprietary data depth into advisor workflows. A projected shortage of 110,000 retiring U.S. advisors by 2034 is compressing the supply of human advice in the $100K–$1M wealth segment, structurally expanding the addressable market for AI-native tools.

key_stat.label: paying customers using Robinhood's AI Strategies tool at ~$250/year average

capability_evidence.evidence: Robinhood's Strategies product is live with 250,000 paying customers and can personalise portfolios around concentrated stock options and home purchase timelines — scenarios earlier robo-advisors could not handle.
capability_evidence.metric: 250,000 paying users at ~$250/year average annual cost

landscape_context.competitor_gap: No peers at this maturity level yet in direct-to-consumer LLM-native advice at this price point

_enrichment.notes: Company not previously in landscape; peer comparison data unavailable for client_personalization segment; maturity assessment based solely on article-derived signals.

_format_errors: week "2026-03-30" is not the Monday of date "2026-03-26" (expected "2026-03-23")

verified_claims: LLM-based financial tools have moved beyond robo-advisor-era ETF bucketing
Robinhood's Strategies product handles complex personalisation scenarios including concentrated stock option positions and home purchase planning
Robinhood's Strategies has 250,000 paying customers at an average $250 per year
More than one-third of consumers across all age groups are now using tools like Claude and ChatGPT for investment guidance
Consumers often consult AI tools before meeting a human advisor
The McKinsey statistic is attributed to Vlad Golyk, coauthor of a McKinsey report on AI and wealth management
Anthropic partnered with LPL Financial
LPL Financial serves approximately 8 million end clients via tens of thousands of registered advisors
Anthropic ingested financial data from S&P Global, Morningstar, and PitchBook
A projected shortage of 110,000 retiring U.S. advisors by 2034
The $100K–$1M wealth segment is identified as the structural opportunity for AI-native tools

The entry frames Anthropic as 'embedding proprietary data depth into advisor workflows' — this is an interpretive paraphrase of the data partnerships, not directly contradicted
_governance.notes: All key figures and claims in the headline, summary, and key stat are clearly and directly supported by the source article with no contradictions found.
